commands.observation: The `fine_tuning_contract` test binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `job_lifecycle_checkpoint_listing_and_permission_admin_semantics`.
The `fine_tuning_controls_contract` test binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `method_variants_and_job_controls_remain_distinct`.

assertions.title: Fine-tuning checkpoints list preserves checkpoint identity and metric envelopes

steps.action: Run `cargo test --test fine_tuning_contract -- --nocapture` in the assigned isolated target dir
Map the passing test to the assertion by inspecting `tests/fine_tuning_contract.rs`
steps.expected: The test directly proves checkpoint identity, `step_number`, and metrics preservation on the list response
steps.observed: The test asserts checkpoint `id` at line 79, `fine_tuned_model_checkpoint` at lines 80-83, `step_number == 4` at line 84, `metrics.train_loss == Some(0.42)` at line 85, and unknown metric preservation via `metrics.extra["custom_metric"] == 0.99` at lines 87-90 after hitting `/v1/fine_tuning/jobs/ftjob_123/checkpoints?after=ckpt_000&limit=1` at line 143.

assertions.title: Fine-Tuning Job Controls and Method Variants Remain Distinct

steps.action: Run `cargo test --test fine_tuning_controls_contract -- --nocapture` in the assigned isolated target dir
Map the passing test to the assertion by inspecting `tests/fine_tuning_controls_contract.rs`
steps.expected: The test directly proves supervised, DPO, and reinforcement create serialization plus typed list-events, pause, and resume behavior
steps.observed: The test constructs `FineTuningMethod::Supervised` at line 33, `FineTuningMethod::Dpo` at line 56, and `FineTuningMethod::Reinforcement` at line 80; then asserts serialized method types `supervised`, `dpo`, and `reinforcement` at lines 150, 161, and 176. It also proves typed event parsing with `events.output.data.len() == 2` plus `FineTuningJobEventLevel::Info` and message `job metrics available` at lines 114-116, and confirms pause/resume lifecycle results via `paused.output.status == "paused"` and `resumed.output.status == "running"` at lines 123 and 130 after asserting the `/events`, `/pause`, and `/resume` routes at lines 138, 142, and 146.

evidence.network: Mocked HTTP route assertions prove `/v1/fine_tuning/jobs/ftjob_reinforcement/events?after=evt_000&limit=2`, `/pause`, and `/resume`.

frictions.description: Cargo output only names the passing test functions, so precise assertion mapping still required source inspection.
frictions.resolution: Reviewed the two assigned test files and tied the passing test names to the exact assertion lines proving `step_number`, DPO serialization, typed list-events, pause, and resume.

summary: Retest succeeded: both assigned mocked cargo commands passed, VAL-ADV-002 is now directly proven by an explicit `step_number` assertion in `fine_tuning_contract`, and VAL-ADV-013 is now directly proven by DPO create serialization plus typed list-events, pause, and resume assertions in `fine_tuning_controls_contract`.
